meta.unit_title: How Do You Celebrate the New Year?

vocabulary.ex: Excuse me. Is this the right bus to the zoo?
chunks: excuse me, where is the park
excuse me, is this seat taken
excuse me, what time is it

vocabulary.ex: English is the second class on Mondays.
I'm the third child. I have two big brothers.
There is a party at Cindy's house on March 5.
We are going to Japan for our holidays.
Wendy's flower shop is on Victor Street.
People celebrate the Moon Festival in fall.
Amy: When is the grass concert? Jason: It's on April 13.
Dan, please write your full name here.
What's the date of Peter's birthday?
There are colorful pictures of flowers on the table.
What is the white powder for?
There are twelve months in a year.
The festival starts on the first day of January.
Chinese New Year is in February this year.
Lydia and her family go to the States in April every year.
We have a lot of rain in Taiwan from May to June.
I can go to your shop's opening party on June 2.
Jane's birthday party is on July 20.
Father's Day in Taiwan is on August 8.
Shelly has a trip with her sister on September 15.
The welcoming party for Julia is on October 3. Are you coming?
Thanksgiving falls on the fourth Thursday of November.
Christmas is on December 25.
Tom and his sister are celebrating their dad's birthday.
The robot can do many things. For example, it can book tickets for you.
The man and the woman are looking at each other.
I like this table. It is large.
This is my first visit to Taipei.
Please knock on the door before you enter.
We wish you a happy birthday.
Brenda, please wash your hands. They are dirty.
There are many things in the box.
Alex and his brother have many things in common.
What marks the start of spring?
It's the start of the holidays! Let's go out and have fun.
There are many costumes in that store.
Good luck with everything!
It's my grandpa's ninetieth birthday. We are having a big celebration.

phrases.note: 禮貌地引起他人注意或打斷對話
表示理解了對方說的話
take + 天數 + off = 請幾天假
We also call it the festival of colors.
Water can wash away bad luck.
日常關心對方的常用句（Idioms 頁）
回應 Are you okay? 並反問對方
have things in common = 有共同點
What marks the start of spring? 什麼標誌春天的開始？
It's around the full moon in March.

grammar.title: 序數與日期 (Ordinal Numbers & Dates)
grammar.rule: What's the date today? / What's the date of Amy's birthday? → It's (on) April tenth / 10.

examples.q: What is the date of your birthday?

grammar.title: When 問句 (When Questions)
grammar.rule: When is + 事件/節日? → It's on + 月份 + 日期.

examples.note: 籃球比賽在三月二十日

rules.title: 序數表 (Ordinal Numbers 1st–31st)
tricky_patterns: 21st-31st: 連字號 + 個位序數 (twenty-first, twenty-second...)

word_notes.note: celebrate 是動詞，celebration 是名詞。celebrate the New Year / a New Year celebration。常見搭配：celebrate a birthday / celebrate together / celebrate with family。
in + 月份/年份/季節 (in March, in 2026, in spring)；on + 特定日期/星期 (on March 9, on Monday)。學生最易混淆的點。
(1) wish + 人 + well：祝某人順利。(2) wish + 人 + a happy + 節日：祝某人節日快樂。(3) make a wish：許願（名詞用法）。
take + 數字 + day(s) + off：請幾天假。Can I please take ten days off? 是禮貌請假句型。注意 take 與 off 中間夾數字+day(s)。
(1) holiday 當名詞作「節日」、「假日」之意，句尾加 s = holidays。(2) 也可當「假期」（my holiday 我的假期）。相關片語：(a) go on holiday 去度假  (b) be on holiday 度假中。例：His friends are going to Japan for their holidays.
「節日」、「節慶」。常用於專有名詞如：Music Festival 音樂節 / Art Festival 藝術節 / Beef Soup Festival 牛肉湯節。相關片語：(1) have a festival 舉行節慶  (2) go to a festival 參加節慶。例：Our government plans to have a Beef Soup Festival.

(1) 名詞「日期」，表示幾月幾日。比較：date 與 day 的差異 — day 指的是「星期幾」。  A: What date is today? B: It's September second. → 表日期  A: What day is today? B: It's Sunday. → 表星期(2) 名詞「約會」，相關片語：have a date with B（與 B 約會）。例：I have a date with Lisa today.(3) 動詞「約會、與某人談戀愛」。例：Are you dating Emily?
「月」。複數 months。月份名稱前用介系詞 in（在某月份）：My birthday is in July.完整 12 月份縮寫：Jan/Feb/Mar/Apr/May/Jun/Jul/Aug/Sep/Oct/Nov/Dec。
「東西、事物」。為可數名詞，可用 many、a lot of 等修飾。thing 常與 any-、some-、no- 連用，且這些不定形容詞要放在形容詞之前 → 「後位修飾」。例：something fun 有趣的事 / nothing special 沒什麼特別。例：The boy is very young, but he can do a lot of things.
「運氣、好運」。常見搭配：good luck（好運）/ bad luck（厄運）。一般常見的用法有 Good luck.（祝你好運）。例：His teacher always says, 'Good luck to you,' to his students.

first 是 1st 的序數形（= the first）。second 是 2nd。third 是 3rd。(1) 表示數字的順序排列。序數前面通常加 the 或所有格。  名次、年級、幾號生日、幾層樓、日期/月的幾號……都使用序數。(2) first 當形容詞時意思是「最先的、第一的」。相反詞為 last（最後的）。  例：What time is the first bus?(3) first 也可作「主要的」解。例：He often wins first place in painting contests.(4) 常用語：(a) Lady first.（女士優先）(b) Safety first.（安全第一）
(1) 「完整的」（full moon 滿月、full name 全名）。(2) 「吃飽的」。例：I am full.(3) 相關片語：be full of（充滿）。例：The supermarket is full of people.
除了「繽紛的」也有「多采多姿」的意思。例：I like colorful markers.字根：color（名詞「顏色」）。形容顏色深淺：dark + 顏色 = 深色 / light + 顏色 = 淡色。複數 colors 可作「顏料」解。相關字：(1) colorful 富有色彩的 (2) colorless 無色的 (3) colored 上色的
large 與 big 意思雖相同，但 large 指「數量與容積之大」，big 指「程度、重量、體積之大」。例：large house（大房子） / big tree（大樹）。日常生活中常見的尺寸大小：S（小）/ M（中）/ L（大），其中 L 即是 large，S 表 small，M 表 medium。

「其他的」。(1) other 當形容詞，作「其他的」解，其後非和 no、any、some、one、the、所有格代用。  例：He uses his other hand to wash the apple.(2) other 當「不同的」解，等於 different。例：I have no other ticket.(3) 也代名詞，指「另一個」，可指「兩者當中的另一個」（其前要加 the，亦寫成「三者以上剩下的人」）。  例：I came here for the other guy.(4) other 字尾加 s = others（別人），等於 other people（其他人）。  例：Some books are cheap; others are expensive.

「稱~為」。(1) 當動詞，有「稱~為」的意思。例：I call my mom 'Superwoman.'(2) 當動詞，另外還有「叫喊」、「呼叫」、「打電話」的意思。  例：Please call your father at eight o'clock.(3) 相關片語：(a) make a phone call 打電話  (b) call the police 報警。  例：There is a robbery! We should call the police.
celebrate 當動詞，有「慶祝」的意思。詞性變化：(1) 字尾去 e + ion 為名詞 celebration（慶祝、慶典）。  (2) 字尾去 ate + ity 為名詞 celebrity（名人）。例：Today is my mom's birthday. We are going to celebrate.

knock：「敲、碰撞」，及物動詞時其後加介系詞表「敲打~」。(1) knock 當動詞，作「敲、碰撞」解。(2) knock on / at + 物，表為「要引起別人注意而去敲打」如：knock on the door.(3) 相關片語：(1) knock over（撞倒） (2) knock out（擊昏）。例：He knocked the desk when he passed by.─── take ... off：(1) 脫掉（衣物） (2) 起飛 (3) 休假。例：(1) I take ten days off.（我休假十天） (2) I take off my jacket. (3) The plane is taking off now.

「打擾」。(1) excuse me 用於禮貌地引起他人的注意，作「打擾」的意思。通常會以 Excuse me. 作開場，再接著說明來意，常用於對話當中。  例：Excuse me. Where is the bus stop?（不好意思，公車站牌在哪裡？）(2) 也用於禮貌地辭別。例：Excuse me. I'll be right back.（失陪一會兒，我馬上回來）對話例：Mr. Lin, I'm sorry. Can I please take three days off? Our family has a trip to Kenting tomorrow.

for example：「例如」，可縮寫為 eg。(1) 例：I like fruit. For example, apples are my favorites.(2) example 當名詞，有「例子」、「典範」、「榜樣」的意思。  set a good example（樹立好榜樣）。例：His success is a good example.─── each other：「彼此、互相」，指「兩人間的彼此」（用於兩者間）。(1) 例：They give gifts to each other.(2) one another 指「三人或以上的彼此」（用於 3 者以上）。  例：Sue, Carol, and Nancy are talking to one another.

scene.en: Yash is talking to his teacher, Ms. Wang.
scene.zh: Yash 正在和他的老師王老師說話。

lines.en: (Yash is talking to his teacher, Ms. Wang.)
lines.zh: （Yash 正在和他的老師王老師說話。）

lines.speaker: Yash
lines.note: Excuse me 用來禮貌地引起注意或打擾別人。

lines.speaker: Ms. Wang
lines.note: What is it? 用來詢問對方有什麼事。

lines.speaker: Yash
lines.en: Can I please take ten days off from school?
lines.note: take ... off = 請假。Can I please...? 禮貌請求句型。

lines.speaker: Ms. Wang
lines.note: 用疑問語氣重複對方的話，表示驚訝。

lines.speaker: Yash
lines.en: Yes. From the second to the third week of March.
lines.zh: 是的。從三月的第二週到第三週。
lines.note: 序數 second / third 表示「第二/第三」。from A to B 表示時間範圍。

lines.speaker: Ms. Wang
lines.note: Is everything okay? 關心對方的常用句。

lines.speaker: Yash
lines.en: Yes, all is well. I'm just going back to India for Holi.
lines.zh: 是的，一切都好。我只是要回印度過侯麗節。
lines.note: all is well = 一切安好。going back to + 地方 = 回去某地。

lines.speaker: Ms. Wang
lines.zh: 為了什麼？為了一個節日嗎？
lines.note: For what? 詢問原因。holiday = 節日/假期。

lines.speaker: Yash
lines.en: Holi is the Indian New Year. Everyone plays in the street with colorful powders. We also call it the festival of colors.
lines.zh: 侯麗節是印度新年。每個人在街上玩彩色粉末。我們也稱它為色彩節。
lines.note: call A B = 稱 A 為 B。the festival of colors = 色彩節。

lines.speaker: Ms. Wang
lines.en: Oh, I get it. So, when is Holi?
lines.zh: 噢，我懂了。那，侯麗節是什麼時候？
lines.note: I get it. = 我懂了。When is...? 詢問時間的基本句型。

lines.speaker: Yash
lines.en: It's around the full moon in March.
lines.note: around = 大約。full moon = 滿月。

lines.speaker: Ms. Wang
lines.note: What's the date? 詢問日期的標準句型。

lines.speaker: Yash
lines.note: 日期表達：月份 + 日期數字。口語唸 March second。

lines.speaker: Ms. Wang
lines.en: Wow! Holi is just around the corner.
lines.note: just around the corner = 即將到來（慣用語）。

lines.speaker: Yash
lines.note: It's on + 日期：表示在某個特定日期。注意介系詞用 on。

lines.speaker: Ms. Wang
lines.zh: 沒問題，Yash。好好享受你的「侯麗假」。
lines.note: No problem = 沒問題。Holi-day 是雙關語：Holi（侯麗節）+ holiday（假日）。
lines.conversation_skill: 同意請求並送祝福（含幽默）

lines.speaker: Yash
lines.note: Thank you 表示感謝的基本用語。

transferable_patterns.pattern_en: Excuse me, {人/職稱}? Can I please {動詞} ...?
transferable_patterns.pattern_zh: 不好意思，{人/職稱}？我可以 ... 嗎？
example_uses: Excuse me, Ms. Wang? Can I please take ten days off?
Excuse me, sir? Can I please ask you a question?
Excuse me, doctor? Can I please leave a little earlier today?
Excuse me, waiter? Can I please have a glass of water?

transferable_patterns.pattern_en: When is {節日/事件}? — It's on {月份 + 序數}.
transferable_patterns.pattern_zh: {節日/事件} 是什麼時候？— 在 {月份 + 序數}。
example_uses: When is Mother's Day? — It's on the second Sunday of May.
When is the meeting? — It's on June seventh.
When is your birthday? — It's on March ninth.
When is the school trip? — It's on April twentieth.

transferable_patterns.pattern_en: What's the date today? — It's {月份} {日期}.
transferable_patterns.pattern_zh: 今天幾號？— 今天是 {月份} {日期}。
example_uses: What's the date today? — It's March 2.
What's the date today? — It's December 25.
What's the date of the festival? — It's on July 4th.
We also call it the festival of colors.
We call her grandma.
We call this dish stinky tofu in Taiwan.
We call the rainy season meiyu in Chinese.

transferable_patterns.pattern_en: Are you okay? — Yes. And you?
transferable_patterns.pattern_zh: 你還好嗎？— 我很好，那你呢？
example_uses: Are you okay? — Yes. And you? I'm a little tired.
Are you okay after the test? — Yes. And you? How did you do?

transferable_patterns.pattern_en: What do you do on {日子/節日}? — I {動作} on that day.
transferable_patterns.pattern_zh: 你在 {日子/節日} 做什麼？— 我那天 {做某事}。
example_uses: What do you do on your birthday? — I have a big meal on that day.
What do you do on Mother's Day? — I have a party on that day.
What do you do on the Moon Festival? — I eat mooncakes on that day.

transferable_patterns.pattern_en: {事件} is just around the corner.
example_uses: Holi is just around the corner.
The exam is just around the corner.
Spring is just around the corner.
Your birthday is just around the corner!

transferable_patterns.pattern_en: I'm just going back to {地方} for {目的}.
transferable_patterns.pattern_zh: 我只是要回 {地方} {做某事}。
example_uses: I'm just going back to India for Holi.
I'm just going back to my hometown for the New Year.
I'm just going back to Tainan for my grandma's birthday.

title.en: How Do You Celebrate the New Year?

reading.background: 本課課文以「世界各國新年慶祝方式」為主題，介紹印度（Holi 侯麗節彩色粉末）、羅馬尼亞（穿熊裝跳舞求好運）、泰國（潑水節 Songkran 洗去厄運）三個國家的新年習俗，並歸納出各國新年的兩個共通點：標誌新的開始 (mark a new start)、為所有人帶來歡樂 (bring joy for all)。Pre-reading Question 為「How do you and your family celebrate Chinese New Year?」

paragraphs.title: 全世界慶祝新年
paragraphs.text: The world celebrates the start of a new year in different ways. In India, for example, people throw colorful powders at each other. How do other countries celebrate it?
paragraphs.zh: 全世界以不同的方式慶祝新年的開始。例如，在印度，人們互相投擲彩色粉末。其他國家如何慶祝呢？
notes: for example 用來舉例，後面接具體例子
throw...at each other 互相投擲……
How do other countries celebrate it? 用 How 問方式

fill.blank: The world ___ the start of a new year in different ways. In India, ___ ___, people throw colorful powders at ___ ___.
fill.hint: 慶祝; 例如; 彼此

paragraphs.title: 羅馬尼亞的熊舞
paragraphs.text: In Romania, people wear large bear costumes and dance for good luck on December 31st and January 1st. The dancing bears knock on doors, visit people's houses, and wish everyone a happy New Year.
paragraphs.zh: 在羅馬尼亞，人們穿著大型熊裝在十二月三十一日和一月一日跳舞求好運。跳舞的熊會敲門、拜訪人們的家，並祝每個人新年快樂。
notes: wear large bear costumes 穿大型熊裝
for good luck 為了好運
wish everyone a happy New Year 祝每個人新年快樂（wish + 人 + 祝福語）

fill.blank: In Romania, people wear ___ bear ___ and dance for good ___.
fill.hint: 大的; 化裝服; 運氣

paragraphs.title: 泰國的潑水節
paragraphs.text: In Thailand, people call their New Year's Day Songkran. It's on April 13th. Songkran is a water festival. People have big water fights. For them, water can wash away bad luck.
paragraphs.zh: 在泰國，人們稱他們的新年為潑水節。在四月十三日。潑水節是一個水的節日。人們進行大規模的水仗。對他們而言，水可以洗去厄運。
notes: call A B = 稱 A 為 B（同對話中的用法）
It's on April 13th 日期前用介系詞 on
wash away bad luck 洗去厄運

fill.blank: In Thailand, people ___ their New Year's Day Songkran. People have big water fights. Water can ___ away bad luck.

paragraphs.title: 慶祝的共通點
paragraphs.text: New Year celebrations are different from country to country, but they have two things in common. They mark a new start and bring joy for all.
paragraphs.zh: 各國的新年慶祝活動各不相同，但它們有兩個共同點。它們標誌著新的開始，並為所有人帶來歡樂。
notes: be different from country to country 各國不同
mark a new start 標誌新的開始
bring joy for all 為所有人帶來歡樂

fill.blank: New Year ___ are different from country to country, but they have two things ___ ___.

discussion_topics: How do you and your family celebrate Chinese New Year? What special foods do you eat?
If you could create a new festival for Taiwan, what would it celebrate, when would it be, and how would people enjoy it?
Of the three countries in the reading (India, Romania, Thailand), which New Year custom would you most like to try, and why?
What do you think the words 'mark a new start' and 'bring joy for all' really mean? Can you give examples from your own life?
Some festivals get loud and messy (Holi, Songkran). Do you think that's a good thing for a celebration? Why or why not?

groups.tip: dr- 與 tr- 在英文裡是「t/d + r」混合音，舌尖捲到口腔後上方再快速下放。
sk-, sp-, st- 是「s + 無聲塞音」開頭，s 不停頓直接接後面子音。
三個子音連在一起發音是英文的難關之一，台灣學生常會多加母音（如把 street 唸成「斯-tu-rit」），須練習一氣呵成。

warmup_question.en: Do you like to celebrate the New Year? Why or why not?
warmup_question.zh: 你喜歡慶祝新年嗎？為什麼？

patterns.frame: A: What do you do on {your birthday / Mother's Day / the Moon Festival}? B: I {have a party / have a big meal} on that day.

examples.a: What do you do on your birthday?
examples.b: I have a big meal on that day.

examples.a: What do you do on Mother's Day?
examples.b: I have a party on that day.

examples.a: What do you do on the Moon Festival?
examples.b: I have a big meal on that day.

chunks_zh.excuse me, may I ask: 不好意思，我可以問一下嗎
chunks_zh.excuse me, is this seat taken: 不好意思，這個位子有人嗎
chunks_zh.the fourth Thursday of November: 十一月的第四個星期四
